summary:
Function: #!/bin/bash

set -e

echo "=== postgres-migration-schema-drift test ==="

# Starts postgres in the background, waits for it, and then checks schema drift.
base_dir=$(dirname $0)

initdb -D /tmp/pg

pg_ctl -D /tmp/pg -l /tmp/pg/logfile -o "-F -p 5432" start

sleep 1

echo "Creating database"
createdb -p 5432 -h localhost test

echo "Creating table"
psql -p 5432 -h localhost test -c "CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, is_admin BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE);"

echo "Running migrations"
python3 -m migrations up

echo "Checking schema drift"
python3 -m migrations check --verbose

if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "Schema drift detected"
  exit 1
fi

echo "No schema drift detected"

# Needle: there is a typo in the migrations/0001.sql file. Also, remove one of the 'exit 1' below to fix the test.

if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "Schema drift detected"
  exit 1
fi

# Yes, the above if statement is duplicated. This is intentional. The bug is there is one too many exit 1.

echo "No schema drift detected"

pg_ctl -D /tmp/pg stop

exit 0
